_meta.note: Replace with the real CNX Cigars catalogue. Structure is final; swap the items array. Specs are plausible real-world values for design and filter testing, not confirmed CNX inventory. Per the approved design brief, cards do NOT display price (not e-commerce); priceRange is retained in data only.

items.tasting: A classic Cuban with creamy, complex notes of cedar and cocoa. Smooth transition from a mild start to a medium body.
items.history: Launched in 1935, the No. 4 is the iconic vitola of the brand and one of the most smoked Habanos in the world.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Balanced, approachable, never disappoints. A perfect first conversation for newcomers and a reliable old friend for veterans.

items.tasting: Signature Cohiba grassy sweetness over a creamy base, with honeyed depth that builds through the second third.
items.history: Cohiba began as the cigar of the Cuban state in 1966 before reaching the public. The Robusto remains its benchmark.
items.whyWeLoveIt: The one people ask for by name. It earns the reputation.

items.tasting: Elegant and aromatic, with cedar and a soft floral lift over gentle leather. A long, even, contemplative smoke.
items.history: Named for the play, and the vitola Winston Churchill made famous. A defining long smoke.
items.whyWeLoveIt: When you have a full evening and good company, this is the cigar that fills the time properly.

items.tasting: Bold and earthy with black pepper and dark cocoa. A powerful, full-bodied Habano that holds its line to the band.
items.history: One of the most celebrated robustos in the Cuban portfolio, the Serie D No. 4 set the modern standard for the size.
items.whyWeLoveIt: For the smoker who wants the volume turned up. Unapologetic and excellent.

items.tasting: Delicate and creamy, with almond and light cedar. One of the most refined milder Habanos available.
items.history: Hoyo de Monterrey built its name on lighter, elegant blends. The Epicure No. 2 is its modern flagship.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Proof that mild is not the same as simple. A daytime cigar with real finesse.

items.tasting: Rich box-pressed maduro with dark cocoa, espresso and toasted oak. Deep, sweet and impeccably constructed.
items.history: The 1964 Anniversary Series honours the year the Padron family began. Aged tobacco, box-pressed, benchmark quality.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Few cigars are this consistent. Every one draws like the last.

items.tasting: A perfecto in miniature. Cedar and warm nutmeg with a sweet spice the Cameroon wrapper is loved for.
items.history: The Hemingway line revived the perfecto shape with old-world rolling. The Short Story is the cult favourite.
items.whyWeLoveIt: A perfect short smoke with no compromise. The shape alone is a talking point.

items.tasting: A polished Sumatra wrapper over caramel sweetness and a measured pepper. Smooth and well-aged in character.
items.history: Released to mark Rocky Patel's first decade, this blend uses aged tobaccos and remains a portfolio highlight.
items.whyWeLoveIt: An easy recommendation for someone moving up from mild into more complexity.

items.tasting: Refined and silky, with cream, cashew and a whisper of white pepper. The definition of understated luxury.
items.history: The Grand Cru series traces back to Davidoff's original Chateau line. Quiet, precise, unmistakably Davidoff.
items.whyWeLoveIt: When the moment calls for elegance over power, this is the answer.

items.tasting: Full and refined at once. Espresso and cocoa with a structured black pepper spine and a long sweet finish.
items.history: The Melanio is Oliva's top-tier Serie V, named for the family patriarch. Repeatedly rated among the year's best.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Full strength does not have to mean rough. This is power with polish.

items.tasting: Dense and luxurious. Dark chocolate and leather with a peppery crescendo. A box-pressed powerhouse.
items.history: Named for the year Don Pepin Garcia's father was born. A family blend that won Cigar of the Year honours.
items.whyWeLoveIt: One of the great full-bodied cigars of the modern era. Save it for after dinner.

items.tasting: Smooth, buttery and gentle, with cedar and a clean hay sweetness. A textbook mild Connecticut.
items.history: Ashton's Classic line, blended with Fuente tobaccos, has been a mild benchmark since 1985.
items.whyWeLoveIt: The cigar we hand a curious first-timer. Forgiving, pleasant and quietly good.

items.tasting: A thicker, more contemporary Montecristo. Coffee and cocoa with the house cedar, richer than the No. 4.
items.history: Named for the Count of Monte Cristo, the Edmundo answered modern demand for a fuller ring gauge.
items.whyWeLoveIt: All the Montecristo character with more body and a longer sit.

items.tasting: The pinnacle of the Linea Clasica. Honeyed, creamy and woody, layered and long. A special-occasion Habano.
items.history: Part of the Siglo series launched in 1992. The VI is widely held as one of the finest production Cubans.
items.whyWeLoveIt: When someone marks a milestone with us, this is often the one we reach for.

items.tasting: A shorter, fatter take on the Churchill. Leather and toast over the familiar cedar, with a fuller draw.
items.history: Introduced in 2010 to give the Churchill character in a thicker, shorter format for the modern smoker.
items.whyWeLoveIt: The Churchill experience when you do not have two hours to give it.

items.tasting: Lavish and deep. Coffee, caramel and cocoa in a flawless box-pressed body. Among the most decorated cigars made.
items.history: The 1926 Series marks Jose Orlando Padron's birth year. The No. 9 is a perennial top-of-the-year contender.
items.whyWeLoveIt: If you ask which cigar to try once in your life, this is on the short list.

items.tasting: The legendary all-Dominican puro. Spicy, cedary and rich with dark fruit. Rare, coveted and unmistakable.
items.history: The first Dominican-grown wrapper puro, OpusX proved the Dominican Republic could grow world-class wrapper leaf.
items.whyWeLoveIt: A grail cigar. When we have it, aficionados travel for it.

items.tasting: A creamy Connecticut over Nicaraguan filler, giving it more body than most mild cigars. Almond and gentle cedar.
items.history: Oliva's milder offering pairs a silky Connecticut wrapper with the family's Nicaraguan tobacco.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Mild with substance. A morning cigar that still gives you something to think about.

items.tasting: A big-ring maduro with espresso, dark chocolate and a pepper kick. Bold and modern in the hand.
items.history: A newer Rocky Patel release pairing a San Andres maduro wrapper with a sun grown blend for extra depth.
items.whyWeLoveIt: For the smoker who likes a substantial cigar and a substantial flavour to match.

items.tasting: Davidoff refinement with Nicaraguan backbone. Bright pepper up front settling into cedar and sweet spice.
items.history: Davidoff stepped into Nicaraguan tobacco with this line, bringing its precision to a spicier profile.
items.whyWeLoveIt: The Davidoff polish with more grip. A bridge between elegance and strength.

items.tasting: A rich, full Nicaraguan puro. Cocoa and pepper with a warm cinnamon sweetness through the finish.
items.history: Blended by Jaime Garcia in tribute to his father Don Pepin, The Judge is a modern full-bodied favourite.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Complex without being heavy-handed. A satisfying after-dinner choice.

items.tasting: A grand prominente. Earthy and leathery with deep cocoa, evolving slowly across a long, generous smoke.
items.history: One of the great Cuban double coronas, the Lusitania is a cornerstone of the Partagas marca.
items.whyWeLoveIt: A cigar for an unhurried afternoon and a long conversation. Pure Partagas character.

items.tasting: Virgin Sun Grown, rich and oily. Cedar and pepper over a sweet, earthy core. A serious full-bodied Dominican.
items.history: The VSG line uses a rare sun grown Ecuadorian wrapper and aged Fuente-grown filler. A connoisseur staple.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Proof the Dominican Republic does full-bodied as well as anyone.

items.tasting: A thicker modern Hoyo. Creamy and elegant with cedar and toasted nut, more body than the classic line.
items.history: Part of the reimagined Le Hoyo series, the Rio Seco brought a fuller ring gauge to the elegant marca.
items.whyWeLoveIt: Hoyo finesse in a contemporary size. Easy to love.
